content:
# Final Review Planner Positions
**Producer:** agent:claude/claude-opus-4-6

## Summary

Investigated the bead ID canonicalization mismatch thoroughly. The amendment identifies a real bug: the selection path (run.rs:588-596) canonicalizes bead IDs to the qualified form (e.g., 'ms-alpha.bead-2'), but the execution path (run.rs:1018-1022) passes the raw bead ID from task_source.bead_id (populated from br show, e.g., 'bead-2') to validate_active_context_alignment (controller.rs:1339-1348), which does a raw string comparison. This mismatch causes the 'controller is already tracking active bead ... and cannot adopt ...' error. All tests currently mask this because they use pre-qualified bead IDs in TaskSource fixtures.

## Votes

- `fr-9-3f12f18c`: Accept
  Confirmed real bug through code tracing. The selection path (run.rs:590) stores the canonicalized form 'ms-id.bead-x' as active_bead_id in the controller. The execution path (run.rs:1022) passes task_source.bead_id — which comes from bead.id via br show (project.rs:361), the raw unqualified form 'bead-x' — to sync_controller_task_claimed → validate_active_context_alignment (controller.rs:1339-1340), which does current_bead_id != bead_id as a raw string comparison. When br returns unqualified IDs, this comparison fails: 'ms-id.bead-x' != 'bead-x'. Tests mask the bug because all TaskSource fixtures use pre-qualified IDs (e.g., 'ms-alpha.bead-2'). The fix should either (a) stop canonicalizing at selection time, or (b) use milestone_bead_refs_match in the alignment check — option (b) is more robust since it makes the comparison normalization-aware everywhere.
